claims.claim_text: Dragonfly can reduce origin-registry requests from about 1,000 direct pulls to roughly a single seed pull plus peer sharing.
claims.anchor_text: Your registry sees 1 request instead of 1,000.
claims.suggested_rewrite: In large rollouts, a P2P layer can reduce origin-registry traffic from every node pulling directly to only a small number of seed or back-to-source pulls.
claims.rationale: The exact 1-versus-1,000 figure is illustrative, but the cited CNCF post supports the underlying P2P traffic-reduction principle.

claims.claim_text: Dragonfly enables deployments to thousands of nodes in minutes rather than hours.
claims.span_hint: Why This Module Matters, sentence after diagram
claims.anchor_text: Dragonfly makes the impossible possible: deploying to thousands of nodes in minutes, not hours.
claims.suggested_rewrite: At large scale, Dragonfly is designed to cut rollout time substantially by shifting most transfer traffic from the registry to peers.
claims.rationale: The exact time contrast is not directly sourced in-module, but CNCF documents large production performance gains and reduced launch times.

claims.claim_text: Dragonfly was created at Alibaba for Singles' Day scale and 10,000+ node distributions.
claims.rationale: The primary source is an Alibaba-origin article on an off-allowlist domain and directly covers Double 11 plus 10,000-server distribution.

claims.claim_text: Dragonfly is described as CNCF Incubating and as having graduated from sandbox in 2020.
claims.anchor_text: - **CNCF Incubating**: Graduated from sandbox in 2020, widely adopted in production
claims.suggested_rewrite: - **CNCF Graduated**: Dragonfly is now a CNCF Graduated project with broad production adoption.
claims.rationale: The current wording is stale and internally inconsistent with Dragonfly's current CNCF maturity status.

claims.claim_text: Organizations report 90%+ reduction in registry egress bandwidth with Dragonfly.
claims.anchor_text: - **Bandwidth Savings**: Organizations report 90%+ reduction in registry egress bandwidth
claims.suggested_rewrite: - **Bandwidth Savings**: Dragonfly case studies report large reductions in source-registry bandwidth and image-pull cost.
claims.rationale: Allowlisted sources support substantial savings, but not this exact registry-egress statistic as written.

claims.claim_text: Dragonfly can distribute more than container images, including files, OCI artifacts, and AI/ML models.
claims.rationale: The upstream README explicitly says Dragonfly improves delivery of files, container images, OCI artifacts, and AI/ML models.

claims.claim_text: Ant Group uses Dragonfly to handle distribution to hundreds of thousands of nodes daily.
claims.anchor_text: - **Ant Group Scale**: Handles distribution to hundreds of thousands of nodes daily
claims.suggested_rewrite: - **Large-Scale Use**: Dragonfly is used for very large production distributions.
claims.rationale: A named-organization scale claim with daily quantitative detail needs a primary source; none was found on the allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Dragonfly integrates with containerd by using registry-mirror style configuration.
claims.span_hint: Configuring Container Runtime Integration, sentence before TOML example
claims.rationale: Official Dragonfly docs describe the containerd integration path, but the docs host is off allowlist.

claims.claim_text: The Dragonfly Helm chart can handle containerd integration automatically when containerRuntime.containerd.enable is set.
claims.span_hint: Configuring Container Runtime Integration, sentence after TOML example
claims.rationale: The cited behavior is documented in official Dragonfly Helm docs, but that source is off allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Dragonfly integrates with Harbor as an upstream registry.
claims.rationale: Official Dragonfly Harbor integration docs exist, but they are hosted on an off-allowlist domain.

claims.claim_text: Dragonfly exposes Prometheus metrics for its components.
claims.span_hint: Monitoring Dragonfly, sentence before ServiceMonitor example
claims.rationale: The official metrics reference is off allowlist even though it directly documents the exported metrics.

claims.claim_text: Dragonfly includes a console for users to operate the system.
claims.rationale: CNCF release notes state that Console v1.0.0 was released and provides a new console for users to operate Dragonfly.

claims.claim_text: Dragonfly supports image preheating before deployment.
claims.rationale: Official Dragonfly manager docs explicitly describe async task management for image preheat, but the docs host is off allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Alibaba needed Dragonfly to deploy updates to 10,000+ nodes during Singles' Day.
claims.rationale: Alibaba's off-allowlist primary account documents Double 11 distribution to over 10,000 servers.

claims.claim_text: Alibaba had to complete the rollout in under 10 minutes.
claims.suggested_rewrite: - Complete quickly enough to be practical for a peak-event rollout
claims.rationale: No primary source was found for this exact timing requirement, so the precise number should be removed.

claims.claim_text: Singles' Day involved traffic spikes of millions of requests per second.
claims.rationale: Alibaba's off-allowlist article gives event-scale request figures, including 42 million database requests per second.

claims.claim_text: Alibaba achieved zero downtime during peak shopping.
claims.suggested_rewrite: - Avoid disruption during the peak shopping period
claims.rationale: This is a real-event reliability claim with no located primary source backing the absolute wording.

claims.claim_text: Dragonfly cut deployment time from 2.7 hours to 5 minutes.
claims.rationale: The exact before-and-after timing was not verified from an authoritative source.

claims.claim_text: Dragonfly reduced registry bandwidth by 95%.
claims.rationale: Allowlisted sources support large bandwidth savings, but not this exact figure for this specific incident.

claims.claim_text: Dragonfly achieved a 99.99% success rate in this Alibaba scenario.
claims.rationale: This exact success-rate number needs a primary source and none was found.

claims.claim_text: Dragonfly later scaled to 100,000+ nodes.
claims.anchor_text: - Scaled to 100,000+ nodes in subsequent years
claims.suggested_rewrite: - The system later expanded to much larger production environments.
claims.rationale: The module gives a precise later scale claim without a verifiable primary source.

further_reading.title: CNCF Dragonfly Project Page
further_reading.why_relevant: Best single allowlisted overview for Dragonfly's current CNCF maturity, scope, and public case-study links.

further_reading.title: Dragonfly Upstream Repository
further_reading.why_relevant: Authoritative upstream source for Dragonfly's stated capabilities and project positioning.

further_reading.title: containerd Registry Host Configuration
further_reading.why_relevant: Useful for understanding the registry-mirror and hosts.toml mechanics that Dragonfly integrates with for container runtimes.

notes: Code blocks, tables, and quiz items were intentionally excluded per schema. Repeated summary claims in the Key Takeaways section were not duplicated when they merely restated earlier prose claims. Several Dragonfly-specific operational details are documented on official d7y.io pages, which currently fall outside the allowlist, so those entries were marked needs_allowlist_expansion.
